summary: DHH expresses admiration for Amazon.com's continuous product improvements, specifically highlighting a new feature showing sample book pages. As a non-US customer shopping on Amazon.co.uk, he's frustrated that innovations roll out to the American site first and take months to reach international versions. He describes his workaround of browsing on the US site but purchasing on the UK site.
key_points: Amazon's sample pages feature represents meaningful product innovation for book shoppers
International Amazon customers experience a significant delay in receiving new features
The US-first rollout strategy creates a two-tier customer experience
money_quotes: Thanks for the continous improvements, but could you please boost tickle down time on improvements a tad?
key_insight: Even when a company delivers genuine product innovation, frustrating rollout gaps between regions undermine the customer experience for international users.
